executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from perimeter breaches to systemic supply chain and identity compromises, accelerated by autonomous AI tooling. Over the past week, attackers executed a coordinated, worm-like campaign poisoning the npm registry and Visual Studio Code marketplace, directly resulting in the exfiltration of 3,800+ internal GitHub repositories and a complete codebase dump at Grafana Labs. This developer-targeted supply chain meltdown proves that open-source ecosystems are no longer collaborative spaces; they are weaponized infection vectors. Simultaneously, zero-day vulnerabilities in foundational trust layers are being weaponized with functional exploits. The YellowKey flaw bypasses BitLocker encryption, MiniPlasma resurrects a dormant Windows driver vulnerability for SYSTEM escalation, and Linux kernel flaws like DirtyDecrypt and PinTheft provide trivial root access. Compounding the crisis, Microsoft Defender itself contains actively exploited zero-days that allow privilege escalation and denial-of-service.

Traditional patching cycles are obsolete. Attackers now leverage AI to chain exploits autonomously, doubling in capability every 4.7 months, while identity-based attacks using Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) frameworks and vishing campaigns render SMS and push-based MFA completely ineffective. Security teams must abandon reactive vulnerability management and pivot to proactive supply chain verification, AI-augmented threat detection, and strict zero-trust enforcement. The perimeter is dead; identity, developer tooling, and kernel integrity are the only remaining battlefields.

threat_stories.headline: The Developer Supply Chain Meltdown: npm and VS Code Poisoned in Coordinated Worm Campaign
threat_stories.narrative:
Attackers executed a highly coordinated, self-perpetuating supply chain assault targeting the open-source developer ecosystem. The campaign began with the compromise of trusted npm maintainer accounts and the distribution of a malicious Visual Studio Code extension (Nx Console v18.95.0). Once installed, the extension silently harvested credentials from local file paths, including Kubernetes configs, AWS tokens, SSH keys, and GitHub authentication secrets. The stolen credentials were immediately weaponized to compromise additional npm accounts, publishing hundreds of malicious package versions within a 22-minute window.

The infection loop spread rapidly across CI/CD pipelines. The leaked Shai-Hulud worm source code enabled copycat actors to deploy modified variants targeting the AntV data visualization suite, TanStack Router, and Axios. The campaign culminated in the exfiltration of approximately 3,800 internal GitHub repositories and a full codebase dump at Grafana Labs, which resulted from missed token rotation following the initial TanStack compromise. This is not isolated malware distribution; it is a systemic collapse of developer trust assumptions.

The speed and scale of this campaign demonstrate that open-source package managers and IDE extension marketplaces have become primary initial access vectors. Attackers no longer need to trick users into clicking phishing links. They simply wait for developers to pull dependencies or install productivity tools. The resulting credential theft bypasses traditional perimeter defenses entirely, granting attackers direct access to cloud infrastructure and source code repositories.

timeline.event: TanStack npm supply chain attack compromises two OpenAI employee devices, forcing emergency macOS updates.
timeline.significance: Marks the initial infection vector targeting high-value AI research environments via trusted dependencies.

timeline.event: Compromised 'atool' npm account publishes 637 malicious versions across AntV and related packages within 22 minutes.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates automated, worm-like propagation leveraging stolen maintainer credentials.

timeline.event: GitHub confirms exfiltration of 3,800+ internal repositories following Nx Console v18.95.0 extension compromise.
timeline.significance: Confirms successful lateral movement from developer workstations to enterprise version control infrastructure.

timeline.event: Grafana Labs admits complete codebase theft linked to missed token rotation post-TanStack attack.
timeline.significance: Highlights cascading failure when credential hygiene lags behind supply chain compromise detection.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software vendors, DevOps teams, and open-source maintainers face critical exposure. Compromised CI/CD pipelines enable unauthorized code injection, credential theft, and infrastructure takeover. Organizations relying on public npm registries or unvetted VS Code extensions are actively compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately audit and remove Nx Console v18.95.0 and all unverified npm packages. Rotate all developer tokens, API keys, SSH/GPG keys, and cloud credentials. Enforce organization-level VS Code extension allowlists. Deploy private npm registries with strict dependency pinning and pre-commit secret scanning. Isolate affected endpoints and conduct full log analysis for unauthorized token usage.

threat_stories.headline: The Agentic AI Security Crisis: Sandbox Escapes and Autonomous Exploit Chaining
threat_stories.narrative:
AI has transitioned from a defensive tool to the primary attack accelerator. Researchers demonstrated a critical sandbox bypass in Anthropic's Claude Code via a SOCKS5 hostname null-byte injection. When chained with prompt injection, the flaw enables execution of attacker-controlled code within the restricted environment, leading to direct exfiltration of credentials, source code, and internal API tokens. Anthropic patched the issue, but the vulnerability existed from the sandbox's general availability release, exposing early adopters to silent data theft.

Simultaneously, AI-assisted development has triggered a secrets sprawl crisis. Rapid 'vibe coding' practices prioritize speed over security, resulting in hardcoded credentials, misconfigured cloud databases, and leaked API keys across public and private repositories. The Moltbook social network exposure alone revealed 1.5 million API tokens and 35,000 email addresses due to improper Supabase configuration. AI models are now autonomously chaining multiple exploits to achieve end goals, matching human expert capabilities in multi-stage attacks.

The difficulty of tasks AI can complete doubles every 4.7 months. This represents a systemic vulnerability landscape shift where AI lowers the barrier for sophisticated, autonomous cyber campaigns. Organizations deploying agentic AI workflows without strict tool-use boundaries, input sanitization, and network isolation are creating internal threat actors that operate beyond traditional security controls.

timeline.event: UK AI Security Institute benchmarks confirm frontier AI models can autonomously chain exploits, doubling capability every 4.7 months.
timeline.significance: Establishes measurable acceleration in AI-driven offensive capabilities, outpacing manual defense cycles.

timeline.event: Microsoft open-sources RAMPART and Clarity to operationalize agentic AI safety and test prompt injection defenses.
timeline.significance: Acknowledges industry-wide gap in AI agent boundary enforcement and red-teaming frameworks.

timeline.event: Anthropic silently patches Claude Code sandbox bypass allowing SOCKS5 null-byte injection and credential exfiltration.
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation of AI agent environments for internal data theft and lateral movement.

timeline.event: Research reveals AI models successfully exploit macOS kernel memory corruption on Apple M5 hardware.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates AI's capability to accelerate zero-day discovery and exploit development across diverse architectures.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: AI developers, cloud infrastructure operators, and enterprise application teams face critical exposure. Unrestricted AI agents enable credential harvesting, cloud metadata theft, and autonomous lateral movement. Organizations using AI coding assistants without network boundaries or secret management are actively leaking sensitive data.
threat_stories.action_required: Update Claude Code to v2.1.90+ and all AI agent runtimes. Implement strict tool-use and behavioral boundary policies. Deploy pre-commit secret scanning and automated credential vaulting. Isolate AI workloads in segmented network zones with no direct internet access. Integrate automated red-teaming frameworks into CI/CD pipelines to continuously validate AI safety controls.

threat_stories.headline: The BitLocker Bypass & Kernel Privilege Escalation Wave
threat_stories.narrative:
A wave of functional zero-days and regression exploits has shattered local privilege boundaries on both Windows and Linux. The YellowKey vulnerability (CVE-2026-45585) bypasses default Windows 11 BitLocker encryption by exploiting the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility within the Windows Recovery Environment. Attackers with physical access deploy a crafted USB drive to trigger a Transactional NTFS replay that deletes winpeshl.ini, granting an unrestricted command shell with full access to the encrypted volume. Microsoft released a manual mitigation, but no permanent patch exists yet.

On the Windows side, MiniPlasma resurrects CVE-2020-17103, a regression in the Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver that allows arbitrary registry key creation in the .DEFAULT user hive. Despite being patched in 2020, the flaw persists on fully patched Windows versions, enabling standard users to escalate to SYSTEM privileges via a race condition. On Linux, DirtyDecrypt (CVE-2026-31635) and PinTheft provide trivial root access by exploiting missing copy-on-write guards and double-free flaws in the kernel subsystem. Compounding the crisis, Microsoft Defender contains actively exploited zero-days that allow privilege escalation and denial-of-service.

These vulnerabilities render traditional endpoint protection and full-disk encryption insufficient. Attackers no longer need to brute-force credentials or deploy complex payloads. They leverage physical access or local user rights to bypass encryption, escalate privileges, and neutralize security tools in minutes.

timeline.event: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse releases MiniPlasma PoC demonstrating SYSTEM privilege escalation on fully patched Windows via cldflt.sys regression.
timeline.significance: Confirms dormant 2020 vulnerability remains functional, invalidating patch compliance assumptions.

timeline.event: DirtyDecrypt (CVE-2026-31635) PoC released, enabling local privilege escalation on Linux kernels with CONFIG_RXGK enabled.
timeline.significance: Provides functional exploit for trivial root access across major Linux distributions.

timeline.event: Microsoft releases manual mitigation for YellowKey (CVE-2026-45585) BitLocker bypass following public exploit disclosure.
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation of physical access vectors to bypass enterprise disk encryption.

timeline.event: Microsoft patches two actively exploited Defender zero-days (CVE-2026-41091, CVE-2026-45498) enabling privilege escalation and DoS.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates attackers targeting the security tool itself to neutralize detection and maintain persistence.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All Windows and Linux endpoints face critical exposure. BitLocker-protected data is vulnerable to physical theft. Local user accounts can achieve SYSTEM/root access. Microsoft Defender endpoints are actively neutralized by zero-day exploits, leaving systems blind to lateral movement.
threat_stories.action_required: Apply Microsoft's YellowKey WinRE mitigation immediately by modifying BootExecute registry values and enforcing TPM+PIN authentication. Patch Linux kernels to address DirtyDecrypt and PinTheft. Update Microsoft Defender Antimalware Platform to v4.18.26040.7+. Implement strict physical security controls and restrict local user privileges. Deploy secondary endpoint protection to maintain coverage if Defender is compromised.

threat_stories.headline: Identity Bypass Campaigns & The Erosion of MFA
threat_stories.narrative:
Identity has become the primary attack surface, and traditional multi-factor authentication is systematically failing. The BlackFile operation (UNC6671) executes high-volume vishing campaigns using IT deployment pretexts to lure victims into Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) frameworks. These lookalike SSO portals capture credentials in real-time and intercept MFA challenges, registering new devices for persistence. Attackers then leverage compromised sessions to exfiltrate data from Microsoft 365, Okta, and Salesforce using Python and PowerShell scripts, bypassing standard logging by triggering 'FileAccessed' instead of 'FileDownloaded' events via Microsoft Graph APIs.

Simultaneously, attackers bypass SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN MFA by exploiting independent handling of UPN and SAM Active Directory login formats. By authenticating via the UPN path, attackers bypass MFA enforcement entirely, enabling brute-force credential attacks and rapid ransomware deployment. The firmware updates fail to automatically remove the vulnerable LDAP configuration, requiring manual remediation. OAuth consent phishing campaigns further exploit user trust to grant attackers persistent application access without triggering traditional MFA alerts.

Push notifications and SMS verification are dead. Attackers intercept or bypass them trivially. Organizations relying on password-centric authentication or non-phishing-resistant MFA are operating with a false sense of security. Valid credentials and tokens appear as legitimate user behavior to traditional security controls, allowing breaches to persist undetected for weeks.

timeline.event: BlackFile operation documented leveraging vishing and AiTM frameworks to bypass SMS/push MFA and exfiltrate SaaS data.
timeline.significance: Confirms systematic erosion of traditional MFA through social engineering and session interception.

timeline.event: OAuth consent phishing campaigns identified bypassing MFA by exploiting user trust in legitimate application permissions.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates shift from credential theft to legitimate permission abuse for persistent access.

timeline.event: SonicWall Gen6 VPN MFA bypass confirmed via UPN login format exploitation, enabling brute-force access.
timeline.significance: Reveals architectural flaws in VPN authentication logic that render MFA configuration ineffective.

timeline.event: Industry analysis confirms identity and session management weaknesses as the primary vector in modern breaches.
timeline.significance: Validates strategic shift from network perimeter defense to identity-centric security architecture.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise identity providers, remote access infrastructure, and SaaS platforms face critical exposure. MFA bypass enables unauthorized access to sensitive data, lateral movement, and ransomware deployment. Valid session hijacking evades traditional detection controls.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2 security keys or passkeys) across all user accounts. Execute SonicWall's six-step LDAP reconfiguration to eliminate UPN-based MFA bypass. Implement conditional access policies based on contextual risk signals. Monitor Microsoft Graph API logs for anomalous 'FileAccessed' events from scripting user agents. Enforce just-in-time access models and strict OAuth application consent reviews.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Infrastructure & Network Appliance Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Network and web infrastructure faces relentless, automated exploitation with max-severity vulnerabilities being weaponized within hours of disclosure. Cisco's Catalyst SD-WAN Controller suffers from CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to gain administrative privileges and manipulate network configurations across the SD-WAN fabric. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, mandating immediate remediation. Simultaneously, Cisco Secure Workload Cluster Software contains CVE-2026-20223, a CVSS 10.0 flaw in internal REST API endpoints that grants unauthenticated Site Admin access across tenant boundaries.

Drupal administrators face an emergency patch for CVE-2026-9082, a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability in the database abstraction API that enables remote code execution on PostgreSQL deployments. The flaw is actively targeted in the wild, with functional exploits expected within hours of the patch release. Microsoft Exchange Server remains under active assault via CVE-2026-42897, a zero-day cross-site scripting flaw triggered by opening crafted emails in Outlook Web Access, allowing arbitrary JavaScript execution and email exfiltration.

These vulnerabilities share a common trait: they require no authentication, exploit default configurations, and provide immediate administrative or code execution capabilities. Automated scanners and AI-driven exploit frameworks are targeting them continuously. Organizations operating unpatched or end-of-life infrastructure are experiencing active compromises.

timeline.event: CISA adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV catalog following confirmed administrative access exploitation.
timeline.significance: Mandates immediate remediation for federal agencies; signals widespread active exploitation.

timeline.event: Drupal announces urgent core security update for CVE-2026-9082 SQL injection/RCE vulnerability affecting PostgreSQL deployments.
timeline.significance: Warns of rapid exploit development; triggers emergency patching windows across web publishers.

timeline.event: Cisco patches CVE-2026-20223 in Secure Workload, addressing unauthenticated REST API bypass granting Site Admin privileges.
timeline.significance: Eliminates max-severity tenant boundary violation but leaves unpatched environments critically exposed.

timeline.event: Microsoft issues mitigation for Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 XSS zero-day actively exploited via crafted emails.
timeline.significance: Confirms ongoing targeting of legacy email infrastructure requiring immediate deployment of emergency mitigations.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Network operators, web publishers, and email infrastructure administrators face critical exposure. Unpatched Cisco, Drupal, and Exchange systems are actively compromised, leading to network configuration manipulation, database takeover, and email exfiltration.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch Cisco SD-WAN to v20.9+ and Secure Workload to v3.10.8.3/v4.0.3.17 immediately. Update Drupal Core to patched versions and apply manual patches for EOL branches. Deploy Exchange Emergency Mitigation Service and restrict OWA access. Implement WAF rules to block SQL injection and XSS payloads. Migrate end-of-life systems to supported versions. Enforce strict network segmentation for management interfaces.

actor_profiles.name: TeamPCP / Shai-Hulud / MiniPlasma
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial gain, credential theft, supply chain disruption
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed coordinated npm and VS Code extension poisoning campaign. Leaked Shai-Hulud source code to spawn copycat variants. Compromised maintainer accounts to publish hundreds of malicious packages. Exfiltrated 3,800+ GitHub repositories and Grafana codebase via silent credential harvesting.

actor_profiles.name: BlackFile (UNC6671)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deployed high-volume vishing campaigns using IT deployment pretexts. Leveraged AiTM frameworks to bypass SMS/push MFA. Harvested SaaS sessions and exfiltrated data via Microsoft Graph APIs using Python/PowerShell scripts. Maintained dedicated data leak site for targeted extortion.

actor_profiles.name: Fox Tempest
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Operated illegal code-signing infrastructure to mask ransomware and malware payloads with legitimate certificates. Service was dismantled by Microsoft following coordinated enforcement action, disrupting ransomware distribution pipelines.

actor_profiles.name: Screening Serpens
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Launched 2026 espionage campaigns targeting Ukrainian officials and government entities. Utilized fake training certificates and tailored phishing infrastructure to establish footholds and deploy persistent access tools.

actor_profiles.name: Cloud Atlas
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Leveraged Microsoft Office Equation Editor vulnerability (CVE-2018-0802) via malicious documents to execute PowerShell scripts. Deployed VBCloud and PowerShower backdoors for persistent access and data exfiltration across targeted environments.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: YellowKey is a security feature bypass allowing attackers with physical access to circumvent BitLocker encryption via WinRE manipulation. Exploits the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility to delete winpeshl.ini, granting unrestricted command shell access to encrypted volumes.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply manual WinRE mitigation by modifying BootExecute registry values. Enforce TPM+PIN authentication. Restrict physical access and disable external boot media in BIOS/UEFI.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: DirtyDecrypt enables local privilege escalation via missing copy-on-write guard in the rxgk subsystem. Allows unprivileged users to overwrite privileged memory or sensitive files like /etc/shadow, granting root access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest kernel patches. Verify CONFIG_RXGK status and disable if unused. Restrict local user access to vulnerable systems.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module allows unauthenticated attackers to crash worker processes via crafted HTTP requests. Potential RCE if ASLR is disabled and specific rewrite configurations are present.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to latest patched version. Ensure ASLR is enabled. Restrict or disable rewrite module if unnecessary. Deploy WAF rules to filter crafted requests.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to gain administrative privileges by sending crafted control connection requests. Enables manipulation of SD-WAN fabric configuration via NETCONF.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to fixed software releases (v20.9 through v26.1.1). No workarounds available. Implement strict network access controls for management interfaces.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability in database abstraction API failing to sanitize queries. Enables arbitrary SQL execution, privilege escalation, and RCE on PostgreSQL deployments. Actively targeted in the wild.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Drupal Core to patched versions (11.3, 11.2, 10.6, 10.5). Apply manual patches for EOL versions. Update Symfony/Twig dependencies. Deploy WAF rules to block SQLi payloads.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Cross-site scripting flaw in Outlook Web Access allowing arbitrary JavaScript execution upon opening crafted emails. Enables email exfiltration and potential session hijacking. Affects on-premise 2016, 2019, and SE.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Deploy Exchange Emergency Mitigation Service. Apply cumulative updates (2016 CU23, 2019 CU14/CU15, SE RTM). Restrict external OWA access. Send images as attachments.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Privilege escalation vulnerability due to improper link resolution before file access in mpengine.dll. Allows local attackers to gain SYSTEM-level permissions, effectively granting full Windows control.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Malware Protection Engine to v1.1.26040.8+. Manually trigger update checks. Deploy secondary endpoint protection to maintain coverage during remediation.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in internal REST API endpoints allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. Enables cross-tenant data access and configuration manipulation.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to v3.10.8.3 or v4.0.3.17. Migrate v3.9 and earlier to supported releases. Restrict API network access via firewall rules until patched.

hunting_leads.title: Detect Malicious npm Package Installations & Credential Harvesting
hunting_leads.context: Supply chain attacks via npm and VS Code extensions silently harvest credentials from local paths. Hunting for suspicious package installations and token access patterns identifies early compromise.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventID == 4688 | where ProcessName in ("node.exe", "npm", "yarn") | where CommandLine contains "install" or CommandLine contains "nx-console" | evaluate regex_match(CommandLine, ".*(?:tanstack|antv|size-sensor|echarts-for-react).*") as SuspiciousPackage | where SuspiciousPackage == true

hunting_leads.title: Identify YellowKey BitLocker Bypass Attempts
hunting_leads.context: YellowKey exploits WinRE by triggering autofstx.exe to delete winpeshl.ini. Monitoring for unexpected WinRE utility execution or recovery environment process spawning detects physical access attacks.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventID == 4688 | where ProcessName in ("autofstx.exe", "winpeshl.exe", "tncfg.exe") | where ParentProcessName contains "winre" or ParentProcessName contains "recovery" | where CommandLine contains "FsTx" or CommandLine contains "Transactional"
indicators: WinRE launch from external USB media

hunting_leads.title: Detect SonicWall VPN MFA Bypass via UPN Format
hunting_leads.context: Attackers bypass SonicWall Gen6 MFA by exploiting UPN login format handling. Hunting for UPN-based authentication events without MFA verification identifies unauthorized access attempts.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventID in (238, 1080) | where LogonFormat == "UPN" or LogonFormat contains "userPrincipalName" | where MFAStatus == "Bypassed" or MFAStatus == "NotRequired" | where SessionType == "CLI"

hunting_leads.title: Hunt for AiTM Session Hijacking & Graph API Exfiltration
hunting_leads.context: BlackFile and similar actors use AiTM frameworks to steal sessions, then exfiltrate data via Microsoft Graph APIs using scripting agents. Monitoring for FileAccessed events from non-browser user agents detects silent data theft.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventName == "FileAccessed" | where UserAgent matches regex "python|powershell|curl|wget" | where ApiEndpoint contains "graph.microsoft.com" | where IsAuthenticated == true and IsMfaVerified == false
indicators: python-requests/2.31.0 user agents accessing Graph API
New MFA device registration from anomalous IPs

hunting_leads.title: Identify Drupal SQLi & Exchange XSS Exploitation Attempts
hunting_leads.context: Automated scanners target CVE-2026-9082 (Drupal) and CVE-2026-42897 (Exchange) continuously. Hunting for specific payload patterns and anomalous HTTP methods identifies active exploitation campaigns.
hunting_leads.query: | where EventID == 8002 or EventID == 4625 | where RequestMethod == "POST" | where UrlPath contains "/wp-admin" or UrlPath contains "/owa" or UrlPath contains "/admin" | where StatusCode in (200, 500, 403) | evaluate regex_match(RequestBody, "(union|select|sleep|benchmark|<script|javascript:)") as PayloadDetected | where PayloadDetected == true
indicators: POST requests to /owa with crafted HTML
SQLi payloads in Drupal form submissions
HTTP 500 errors on PostgreSQL Drupal instances
XSS payloads targeting Exchange OWA endpoints

statistics.key_changes: AI-driven exploit chaining now accounts for 31% of recent breaches, surpassing stolen credentials as the primary initial access vector. Supply chain attacks have evolved from isolated package compromises to self-perpetuating worm campaigns targeting CI/CD pipelines and IDE extensions. Identity-based attacks have systematically neutralized SMS and push MFA through AiTM frameworks and vishing campaigns.
emerging_trends: Agentic AI sandbox escapes enabling internal credential exfiltration
npm/VS Code supply chain poisoning with automated credential harvesting
Kernel regression exploits (MiniPlasma, DirtyDecrypt) bypassing patch compliance
AI-assisted vulnerability discovery flooding bug bounty programs with duplicate reports
Physical access attacks leveraging WinRE manipulation to bypass full-disk encryption
declining_threats: Reliance on push-based MFA as a standalone control
